overview: Migrate the Syrian Daily News Bot from AWS Lambda to Cloudflare Workers, replacing AWS AI services with OpenRouter, DynamoDB with Cloudflare D1, S3 with R2, and EventBridge scheduling with Workers Cron Triggers.
goals: Deploy entire application on Cloudflare Workers only
Replace AWS AI (OpenAI/Anthropic) with OpenRouter API
Replace DynamoDB with Cloudflare D1 for state management
Replace S3 with Cloudflare R2 for intermediate storage
Replace EventBridge scheduling with Cloudflare Workers Cron Triggers
Migrate Telegram bot integration to work with Cloudflare Workers runtime
Maintain 5-stage pipeline architecture (Collection → Deduplication → Summarization → Website Publishing → Telegram Posting)
Ensure idempotent pipeline execution with state tracking
Configure quality gates for typecheck, lint, test, build, and deploy
nonGoals: Keep AWS infrastructure (Lambda, DynamoDB, S3, EventBridge)
Change core news collection and processing logic
successMetrics: All pipeline stages execute successfully on Cloudflare Workers
OpenRouter API correctly processes AI requests for summarization and deduplication
D1 database tracks pipeline state correctly
R2 stores intermediate data between pipeline stages
Workers Cron Trigger executes daily collection at 20:01 UTC
Telegram posts appear in both English and Arabic channels
Local development works with wrangler dev
openQuestions: How to handle Cloudflare Workers 10ms CPU time limit for AI processing?
What queue solution to use for stage-to-stage communication?
How to configure D1 database migrations?
How to manage R2 bucket lifecycle for intermediate files?
uiNotes: No UI changes - this is backend-only migration

importFormat.description: Existing data structures remain unchanged - channels.json, news schemas

rules: Pipeline stages must check state before processing to ensure idempotency
Each stage uploads intermediate data to R2 before triggering next stage
Workers Cron runs at 20:01 UTC daily (23:01 Damascus time)
OpenRouter API calls use unified interface for multiple LLM providers
grammY bot must use webhook mode for Cloudflare Workers

stories.title: Set up Cloudflare Workers project with Wrangler
stories.description: As a developer, I want to configure the project for Cloudflare Workers deployment so that it can run on Cloudflare infrastructure.
acceptanceCriteria: Install wrangler as dev dependency: npm install -D wrangler
Create wrangler.toml configuration file
Configure workers.toml with proper name, compatibility_date, and node_compat
Example: wrangler.toml created with name='sy-daily'
Negative case: missing wrangler.toml -> wrangler deploy fails

stories.title: Configure OpenRouter AI provider
stories.description: As a developer, I want to replace AWS AI (OpenAI/Anthropic) with OpenRouter API so that AI requests go through OpenRouter's unified API.
acceptanceCriteria: Update AI provider configuration to use OpenRouter endpoint
Add OPENROUTER_API_KEY to environment variables
Modify src/ai/getLLMProvider.ts to use OpenRouter
Install OpenRouter SDK if needed: npm install @openrouter/ai-sdk
Example: AI_MODEL=openrouter/anthropic/claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022
Negative case: invalid API key -> OpenRouter returns 401 error

stories.title: Set up Cloudflare D1 database for state management
stories.description: As a developer, I want to replace DynamoDB with Cloudflare D1 for pipeline state tracking so that the pipeline can track execution progress.
acceptanceCriteria: Create D1 database: wrangler d1 create sy-daily-db
Add D1 binding to wrangler.toml
Create database schema/migrations for Briefing entity
Install D1 SDK or use @cloudflare/workers-types
Example: D1 database created with schema matching existing DynamoDB
Negative case: missing D1 binding -> workers runtime error

stories.title: Configure Cloudflare R2 for intermediate storage
stories.description: As a developer, I want to replace S3 with Cloudflare R2 for storing intermediate pipeline data so that news data persists between stages.
acceptanceCriteria: Create R2 bucket: wrangler r2 bucket create sy-daily-storage
Add R2 binding to wrangler.toml
Update S3 upload/download functions to use R2 API
Install @cloudflare/r2
Example: R2 bucket created and configured in wrangler.toml
Negative case: invalid R2 credentials -> R2 API error

stories.title: Set up Cloudflare Workers Cron Trigger for daily scheduling
stories.description: As a developer, I want to replace AWS EventBridge with Cloudflare Workers Cron Trigger so that news collection runs daily at 20:01 UTC.
acceptanceCriteria: Configure cron trigger in wrangler.toml: triggers = [{ cron = '1 20 * * *' }]
Update Collect handler to respond to cron events
Test cron trigger locally with wrangler dev --remote
Example: cron '1 20 * * *' = 20:01 UTC daily
Negative case: invalid cron syntax -> wrangler validation error

stories.title: Migrate Telegram bot to grammY with webhook mode
stories.description: As a developer, I want to update the Telegram bot integration to work with Cloudflare Workers so that it can post summaries to channels.
acceptanceCriteria: Update grammy bot configuration for Cloudflare Workers webhook mode
Configure TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET in wrangler.toml secrets
Create /webhook endpoint in workers for Telegram updates
Example: bot uses Bot.api with webhook URL from Workers
Negative case: missing webhook secret -> Telegram rejects webhook

stories.title: Update build configuration for Cloudflare Workers
stories.description: As a developer, I want to update the build process so that TypeScript compiles to Workers-compatible output.
acceptanceCriteria: Update esbuild.config.ts to target 'esnext' and use Cloudflare Workers runtime
Configure package.json type: 'module' if needed
Update build output to single worker script or split by routes
Example: esbuild targets cloudflare pages/functions
Negative case: targeting Node.js runtime -> workers runtime errors

stories.title: Add quality gates to package.json scripts
stories.description: As a developer, I want to ensure all quality gates (typecheck, lint, test, build) pass before deployment so that code quality is maintained.
acceptanceCriteria: Ensure npm run typecheck passes (tsc --noEmit)
Ensure npm run lint passes
Ensure npm run test passes
Ensure npm run build produces valid output
Example: all commands run successfully
Negative case: any command fails -> CI should fail

stories.title: Remove banner generation feature
stories.description: As a developer, I want to remove the banner generation feature since it's out of scope for this migration.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove sharp dependency from package.json
Remove banner-related code from PostToTelegram handler
Update templates to not require banner images
Example: posts no longer include banner images
Negative case: banner code remains -> unnecessary dependencies

stories.title: Deploy to Cloudflare and verify pipeline execution
stories.description: As a developer, I want to deploy the application to Cloudflare and verify the full pipeline executes correctly so that the migration is complete.
acceptanceCriteria: Run wrangler deploy successfully
Verify Workers Cron triggers collection
Check D1 database has briefing records after collection
Check R2 has intermediate JSON files (collected, deduplicated, summarized)
Verify Telegram posts appear in English and Arabic channels
Example: Full pipeline executes end-to-end
Negative case: any stage fails -> diagnose and fix
